title: Evangelion JO vs Battle Orchestra psp
display_title: Evangelion JO vs Battle Orchestra psp
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion JO vs Battle Orchestra psp             Quote Post by eraserhead » Sat Oct 17, 2009 3:37 pm Hi! Im new and would really appreciate the help of the expert opinions on this board. Im looking to buy either Evangelion JO or Battle Orchestra for PSP, but cant decide which one to buy I liked the eva 64 game but havnt had any experience playing any other eva games. I dont speak Japanese, and Id like an interesting game but nothing too ridiculous. Ive spent the last two weeks looking at what reviews/game guides/opinions I can find on the net (including the board posts here), after some time I can see there are pros and cons of both games, some of which as far as I can see are- Battle Orchestra: Pros: Easy/Fun to play, A bit different, Doesnt take itself too seriously Cons: Perhaps a little too ridiculous, Frame rate problems, Stupid AI Evangelion JO: Pros: Solid Game, Lots of Features, combination action/adventure (deeper game) Cons: Difficult to Play, Re-uses up old storyline features from previous game So I was hoping someone who has played both could help me make a decision on which to buy! Please! I realise its subjective and they are completely different games but Id really appreciate any opinions. Fun Factor is important to me, but also a game that Id want to spend time with because its interesting Thanks!!! Any advice would be REAALLLYY appreciated!!

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Sat Oct 17, 2009 4:14 pm You forgot one thing about Jo that should be mentioned.....If you have a PSP with CFW, JO will NOT play even if you have the UMD. And I have had a BITCH of a time getting the ISO of Battle Orchestra to run.

[]:             Quote Post by eraserhead » Sun Oct 18, 2009 3:37 am scarmullet wrote: You forgot one thing about Jo that should be mentioned.....If you have a PSP with CFW, JO will NOT play even if you have the UMD. And I have had a BITCH of a time getting the ISO of Battle Orchestra to run.

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Sun Oct 18, 2009 4:56 am I havent played Jo because of the CFW (maybe this'll be resolved with GEN-C coming out soon), but Battle Orchestra has a Smash Bros feel about it, you know an Arcade party fighter.

[]:             Quote Post by Boredom » Mon Oct 19, 2009 3:16 pm scarmullet wrote: You forgot one thing about Jo that should be mentioned.....If you have a PSP with CFW, JO will NOT play even if you have the UMD. And I have had a BITCH of a time getting the ISO of Battle Orchestra to run.

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Mon Oct 19, 2009 4:57 pm I got Battle Orchestra working on my Gen-B2... Gods be damned that game can be cruel (fighting all nine MP Eva three at a time with no compensation)...on the other hand, Touji's surprisingly fun to play as and I can't wait to unlock Kaworu

[]:             Quote Post by Boredom » Mon Oct 19, 2009 8:55 pm Teague wrote: I got Battle Orchestra working on my Gen-B2... Gods be damned that game can be cruel (fighting all nine MP Eva three at a time with no compensation)...on the other hand, Touji's surprisingly fun to play as and I can't wait to unlock Kaworu

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Oct 19, 2009 9:04 pm I have Eva:Jo for PSP but I've been stuck on the Issy battle. The related thread to that game is here: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=6450

[]:             Quote Post by eraserhead » Tue Oct 20, 2009 4:00 am Boredom wrote: scarmullet wrote: You forgot one thing about Jo that should be mentioned.....If you have a PSP with CFW, JO will NOT play even if you have the UMD. And I have had a BITCH of a time getting the ISO of Battle Orchestra to run.

[]:             Quote Post by Boredom » Tue Oct 20, 2009 2:49 pm can you read wapanese? if so buy Jo if not buy Battle Arkestra

[]:             Quote Post by eraserhead » Tue Oct 20, 2009 5:34 pm Boredom wrote: can you read wapanese? if so buy Jo if not buy Battle Arkestra

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Nov 06, 2009 5:49 pm scarmullet wrote: You forgot one thing about Jo that should be mentioned.....If you have a PSP with CFW, JO will NOT play even if you have the UMD. And I have had a BITCH of a time getting the ISO of Battle Orchestra to run.

[]:             Quote Post by Lleonard Pler » Mon Dec 07, 2009 2:04 pm Why choose Evangelion: Jo or Battle Orchestra, when you have got the impressive "Evangelion 2: Another Cases" on the PSP, which is probably the best Evangelion game ever?

[]:             Quote Post by LordCaldwell » Wed Dec 16, 2009 9:39 am Are any of the psp games avalible in English? I can't read Japanese... :(

[]:             Quote Post by Lleonard Pler » Sun Dec 20, 2009 10:31 am LordCaldwell wrote: Are any of the psp games avalible in English? I can't read Japanese... :(

[]: Evangelion JO vs Battle Orchestra psp             Quote Post by eraserhead » Sat Oct 17, 2009 3:37 pm Hi! Im new and would really appreciate the help of the expert opinions on this board. Im looking to buy either Evangelion JO or Battle Orchestra for PSP, but cant decide which one to buy I liked the eva 64 game but havnt had any experience playing any other eva games. I dont speak Japanese, and Id like an interesting game but nothing too ridiculous. Ive spent the last two weeks looking at what reviews/game guides/opinions I can find on the net (including the board posts here), after some time I can see there are pros and cons of both games, some of which as far as I can see are- Battle Orchestra: Pros: Easy/Fun to play, A bit different, Doesnt take itself too seriously Cons: Perhaps a little too ridiculous, Frame rate problems, Stupid AI Evangelion JO: Pros: Solid Game, Lots of Features, combination action/adventure (deeper game) Cons: Difficult to Play, Re-uses up old storyline features from previous game So I was hoping someone who has played both could help me make a decision on which to buy! Please! I realise its subjective and they are completely different games but Id really appreciate any opinions. Fun Factor is important to me, but also a game that Id want to spend time with because its interesting Thanks!!! Any advice would be REAALLLYY appreciated!!
